1	# Implementation Plan: bids-utils â€” Core Library & CLI
2	
3	**Branch**: `000-initial-design` | **Date**: 2026-04-03 | **Spec**: [000-initial-design.md](../000-initial-design.md)
4	**Input**: Feature specification from `.specify/specs/000-initial-design.md`
5	
6	## Summary
7	
8	Build `bids-utils`, a Python library and CLI for manipulating BIDS datasets. Core operations: file renaming (with sidecar/scans tracking), schema-driven migration (1.x deprecations + 2.0), metadata aggregation/segregation, subject/session renaming, and dataset merge/split. All operations are schema-driven via `bidsschematools`, VCS-aware, and validated against `bids-examples`.
9	
10	## Technical Context
11	
12	**Language/Version**: Python 3.10+ (per spec assumptions)
13	**Primary Dependencies**: `bidsschematools` (schema access), `click` (CLI framework)
14	**Optional Dependencies**: `bids-validator-deno` (testing), `bids2table` (dataset querying, if needed)
15	**Storage**: Filesystem (BIDS datasets are directory trees)
16	**Testing**: `pytest` orchestrated by `tox` (with `tox-uv`)
17	**Target Platform**: Linux, macOS, Windows (cross-platform filesystem operations)
18	**Project Type**: Library + CLI
19	**Performance Goals**: O(n) in affected files, not O(nÂ˛) in total dataset size. Usable on 1000-subject datasets.
20	**Constraints**: Must not corrupt valid BIDS datasets. Must support git/git-annex/DataLad workflows.
21	**Scale/Scope**: Single-developer start, community contributions expected. ~10 CLI commands at maturity.
22	
23	## Constitution Check
24	
25	*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*
26	
27	| Principle | Status | Notes |
28	|-----------|--------|-------|
29	| I. Do No Harm | PASS | Every operation validates affected entities; `--dry-run` mandatory; atomic operations |
30	| II. Schema-Driven | PASS | All BIDS knowledge from `bidsschematools`; multi-version support; FR-033 audit ensures schema/rule sync across all 6 deprecation levels |
31	| III. Library-First | PASS | Every CLI command maps to a public library function |
32	| IV. CLI Excellence | PASS | `--dry-run`, `--json`, `-v`/`-q`, meaningful exit codes for every command |
33	| V. Test-First | PASS | TDD enforced; `bids-examples` sweep testing; randomized testing for coverage |
34	| VI. Performance | PASS | Lazy evaluation; no full-dataset loading for single-entity operations |
35	| VII. VCS Awareness | PASS | Auto-detect git/git-annex/DataLad; use VCS primitives when present |
36	| VIII. Observability | PASS | Structured logging; JSON change manifests; dry-run parity |
37	| IX. Simplicity | PASS | Flat module structure; composition over monoliths; YAGNI |
38	| X. Versioning | PASS | SemVer; automated releases via intuit/auto |
39	| XI. DRY | PASS | Duplication detection in CI (pylint + jscpd) |
40	
41	## Project Structure
42	
43	### Documentation (this feature)
44	
45	```text
46	.specify/specs/000-initial-design/
47	â”śâ”€â”€ plan.md              # This file
48	â”śâ”€â”€ research.md          # Prior art & ecosystem analysis
49	â”śâ”€â”€ data-model.md        # Core data model design
50	â”śâ”€â”€ quickstart.md        # Getting started guide
51	â”śâ”€â”€ contracts/           # Interface contracts
52	â””â”€â”€ tasks.md             # Implementation tasks (via /speckit.tasks)
53	```
54	
55	### Source Code (repository root)
56	
57	```text
58	pyproject.toml           # Single source of truth for deps, metadata, build
59	tox.ini                  # Test orchestration (pytest, lint, type, duplication)
60	mkdocs.yml               # Documentation site config
61	
62	src/bids_utils/
63	â”śâ”€â”€ __init__.py          # Package root, version
64	â”śâ”€â”€ _types.py            # Shared type definitions (PathLike, Entity, etc.)
65	â”śâ”€â”€ _vcs.py              # VCS detection and operations (git mv, git annex, datalad)
66	â”śâ”€â”€ _schema.py           # Schema loading and querying helpers (wraps bidsschematools)
67	â”śâ”€â”€ _io.py               # Content-aware file I/O (annexed content policy enforcement)
68	â”śâ”€â”€ _tsv.py              # Shared TSV read/write utilities (used by _scans.py, _participants.py)
69	â”śâ”€â”€ _scans.py            # _scans.tsv read/write/update operations
70	â”śâ”€â”€ _participants.py     # participants.tsv read/write/update operations
71	â”śâ”€â”€ _sidecars.py         # Sidecar discovery (find all associated files for a BIDS file)
72	â”śâ”€â”€ _dataset.py          # Dataset-level operations (find root, read dataset_description)
73	â”śâ”€â”€ rename.py            # File rename: core operation (Story 1)
74	â”śâ”€â”€ migrate.py           # Schema-driven migration (Stories 2, 3)
75	â”śâ”€â”€ metadata.py          # Metadata aggregate/segregate/audit (Story 6)
76	â”śâ”€â”€ subject.py           # Subject rename/remove (Stories 4, 7)
77	â”śâ”€â”€ session.py           # Session rename/move-into-session (Story 5)
78	â”śâ”€â”€ merge.py             # Dataset merge (Story 9)
79	â”śâ”€â”€ split.py             # Dataset split (Story 10)
80	â”śâ”€â”€ run.py               # Run remove with reindexing (Story 8)
81	â””â”€â”€ cli/
82	    â”śâ”€â”€ __init__.py      # CLI entry point (click group)
83	    â”śâ”€â”€ _common.py       # Shared CLI options (--dry-run, --json, -v/-q, --force)
84	    â”śâ”€â”€ rename.py         # bids-utils rename
85	    â”śâ”€â”€ migrate.py        # bids-utils migrate
86	    â”śâ”€â”€ metadata.py       # bids-utils metadata {aggregate,segregate,audit}
87	    â”śâ”€â”€ subject.py        # bids-utils subject-rename, bids-utils remove
88	    â”śâ”€â”€ session.py        # bids-utils session-rename
89	    â”śâ”€â”€ merge.py          # bids-utils merge
90	    â”śâ”€â”€ split.py          # bids-utils split
91	    â””â”€â”€ run.py            # bids-utils remove-run
92	
93	tests/
94	â”śâ”€â”€ conftest.py          # Shared fixtures (tmp BIDS datasets, bids-examples access)
95	â”śâ”€â”€ test_rename.py       # Unit + integration tests for rename
96	â”śâ”€â”€ test_migrate.py      # Migration tests (multi-version)
97	â”śâ”€â”€ test_metadata.py     # Metadata manipulation tests
98	â”śâ”€â”€ test_subject.py      # Subject operations tests
99	â”śâ”€â”€ test_session.py      # Session operations tests
100	â”śâ”€â”€ test_merge.py        # Merge tests
101	â”śâ”€â”€ test_split.py        # Split tests
102	â”śâ”€â”€ test_run.py          # Run removal tests
103	â”śâ”€â”€ test_io.py           # Content-aware I/O tests (annexed modes)
104	â”śâ”€â”€ test_vcs.py          # VCS integration tests
105	â”śâ”€â”€ test_cli.py          # CLI smoke tests
106	â”śâ”€â”€ test_cli_common.py   # Tests for shared CLI options/decorators
107	â”śâ”€â”€ test_tsv.py          # Tests for shared TSV utilities
108	â””â”€â”€ integration/
109	    â””â”€â”€ test_bids_examples.py  # Sweep tests against bids-examples
110	```
111	
112	**Structure Decision**: Single-project layout with `src/` layout (PEP 517/518 compliant). Library modules at `src/bids_utils/`, CLI as a subpackage. Private modules prefixed with `_` for internal utilities. This is the simplest structure that supports the library-first + CLI wrapper architecture.
113	
114	## Implementation Phases
115	
116	### Phase 0: Project Scaffolding (Foundation)
117	
118	**Goal**: Working project skeleton with CI, linting, type checking, and an empty CLI.
119	
120	**Steps**:
121	1. Initialize project using copier-astral template (or manual setup with uv)
122	2. Configure `pyproject.toml` with dependency layers (test/devel/ci)
123	3. Configure `tox.ini` with envs: py310-py314, lint, type, duplication
124	4. Set up GitHub Actions workflow (invoke tox via tox-gh-actions)
125	5. Configure mkdocs with basic structure
126	6. Create `src/bids_utils/__init__.py` with version
127	7. Create `src/bids_utils/cli/__init__.py` with click group entry point
128	8. Set up intuit/auto for automated releases
129	9. Add `bids-examples` as a git submodule for testing
130	10. Verify: `tox` passes, `bids-utils --help` works, CI green
131	
132	**Dependencies**: None (first phase)
133	
134	### Phase 1: Core Infrastructure (Private Modules)
135	
136	**Goal**: Build the shared utilities that all commands depend on.
137	
138	**Steps** (implement in this order, each with tests first):
139	
140	1. **`_types.py`**: Type definitions â€” `BIDSPath`, `Entity` (key-value pair), `EntitySet`, path-like protocols
141	2. **`_dataset.py`**: Find dataset root (walk up to `dataset_description.json`), read `BIDSVersion`, detect BIDS validity basics
142	3. **`_schema.py`**: Wrap `bidsschematools.schema.load_schema()` â€” load by version, query entities, query suffixes, query sidecar extensions, query deprecation rules
143	4. **`_vcs.py`**: Detect VCS type (none, git, git-annex, datalad). Provide `move()`, `remove()`, `commit()` that dispatch to the right backend. Handle dirty-tree detection.
144	5. **`_sidecars.py`**: Given a BIDS file path, find all associated sidecars by replacing extension with each known sidecar extension (from schema). Handle the case where sidecar might be at a higher level (inheritance).
145	6. **`_scans.py`**: Read/write `_scans.tsv`. Find the scans file for a given file. Update/remove entries by filename.
146	7. **`_participants.py`**: Read/write `participants.tsv`. Add/remove/rename subject entries.
147	
148	**Dependencies**: Phase 0 complete
149	
150	### Phase 1b: Annexed Content Handling (FR-022)
151	
152	**Goal**: All file reads work correctly on git-annex/DataLad datasets via a `--annexed` policy option.
153	
154	**Steps**:
155	1. **Foundation types**: Add `AnnexedMode` enum and `ContentNotAvailableError` to `_types.py`. Add `annexed_mode` field to `BIDSDataset`.
156	2. **VCS protocol extension**: Extend `VCSBackend` protocol with four new methods:
157	   - `has_content(path) -> bool` / `get_content(paths)` â€” for reads
158	   - `unlock(paths)` / `add(paths)` â€” for writes (unlock locked annexed files before modification, re-annex after)
159	   - Implementations: `NoVCS`/`Git` â†’ trivial (always True, no-op for unlock, `git add` for add); `GitAnnex` â†’ check symlink target, `git annex get/unlock/add`; `DataLad` â†’ `datalad get/unlock`, `git annex add`.
160	3. **Content-aware I/O** (`_io.py`):
161	   - `ensure_content(path, vcs, mode)` â€” enforces `--annexed` policy for reads
162	   - `ensure_writable(path, vcs)` â€” unlocks locked annexed files before writes (always, regardless of `--annexed` mode)
163	   - `mark_modified(paths, vcs)` â€” calls `vcs.add()` after writes to re-annex files
164	   - `read_json(path, vcs, mode)` / `write_json(path, data, vcs)` â€” content-aware JSON I/O
165	4. **Wire through existing code**: Update `_tsv.read_tsv`/`write_tsv` with optional VCS/mode params. Update all callers. Replace inline JSON reads/writes in `metadata.py` (~6 read + ~3 write sites) and `migrate.py` (~11 read + ~6 write sites) with `_io` helpers.
166	5. **CLI wiring**: Add `--annexed` to Click group with `envvar="BIDS_UTILS_ANNEXED"`. `load_dataset()` sets `annexed_mode` on the returned `BIDSDataset`.
167	6. **Tests**: Mock VCS tests for all four modes. Unlock/add lifecycle tests. Integration test with real git-annex repo (locked files, content present/absent).
168	
169	**Dependencies**: Phase 1 complete. Can be done at any point after Phase 1, but should be done before real-world usage on annexed datasets.
170	
171	### Phase 1c: Symlink Safety & Dry-Run Detail (FR-003, FR-023, FR-024)
172	
173	**Goal**: Fix the `is_file()` symlink bug that silently skips annexed data files during rename operations. Enhance `--dry-run` to show per-file detail. Add annex operation logging.
174	
175	**Steps**:
176	1. **Symlink bug fix (T092)**: Audit all `is_file()` calls used for file iteration. Replace with `not path.is_dir()` in `session.py`, `subject.py`, `run.py`, `split.py`, `merge.py`, `_sidecars.py`, `migrate.py`. Keep `is_file()` where checking for file existence (not iteration).
177	2. **Annex test fixture (T093)**: `tmp_annex_dataset` in conftest.py â€” git-annex repo with locked symlinks alongside regular files.
178	3. **Regression tests (T094)**: Session/subject/file rename on annexed dataset â€” verify all files including symlinks are renamed.
179	4. **Dry-run detail (T095-T096)**: `--dry-run=overview|detailed`. Update `common_options`, ensure all library functions populate per-file `Change` entries. `output_result` renders overview vs detailed.
180	5. **Annex logging (T097)**: INFO-level logging for get/unlock/add operations in `_io.py`.
181	6. **Tests (T098)**: Verify `--dry-run=detailed` output.
182	
183	**Dependencies**: Phase 1b complete. BLOCKS real-world usage on annexed datasets.
184	
185	### Phase 2: File Rename (Story 1 â€” P1)
186	
187	**Goal**: `bids-utils rename` working end-to-end with full test coverage.
188	
189	**Steps**:
190	1. Implement `rename.py` library function:
191	   - Parse source file path into entities
192	   - Accept entity overrides (e.g., `--set task=nback`)
193	   - Compute new filename from modified entities
194	   - Discover all sidecars for the source file
195	   - Check for conflicts (target already exists)
196	   - Execute renames (filesystem or VCS)
197	   - Update `_scans.tsv` if applicable
198	2. Implement `cli/rename.py`:
199	   - Wire up arguments, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `-v`/`-q`
200	   - Human-readable and JSON output modes
201	3. Tests:
202	   - Unit tests for entity parsing, filename construction
203	   - Integration tests with tmp BIDS datasets
204	   - `bids-examples` sweep: rename a random file, validate dataset
205	
206	**Dependencies**: Phase 1 complete
207	
208	### Phase 3: Migration â€” 1.x Deprecations (Story 2 â€” P1)
209	
210	**Goal**: `bids-utils migrate` handles all known 1.x deprecations with tiered migration levels.
211	
212	**Prior art**: PR #2282's decorator-based migration registry pattern is directly reusable. It implements `@registry.register(name="...", version="1.10.0", description="...")` with dry-run support and JSON-safe operations. Currently handles 3 migrations; bids-utils must extend to cover all 1.x deprecations.
213	
214	**Steps**:
215	1. **Extend MigrationRule schema** (FR-029):
216	   - Add `level` field to `MigrationRule`: `safe` | `advisory` | `non-auto-fixable`
217	   - Add `condition` field for contextual rules (e.g., `AcquisitionDuration` requires `VolumeTiming`)
218	   - This formalizes the migration record schema (analogous to validator schemas, cf. dandi-cli)
219	2. **Implement CLI filtering** (FR-030):
220	   - `--level=safe` (default), `--level=advisory`, `--level=all`
221	   - `--rule-id=ID` / `--exclude-rule=ID` for per-rule control
222	   - `--mode=auto` (default: interactive when PTY, non-interactive otherwise), `--mode=non-interactive`, `--mode=interactive`
223	   - Library API: `level`, `mode`, `rule_ids`, `exclude_rules` parameters on `migrate_dataset()`
224	3. Implement migration rule engine in `migrate.py`:
225	   - Scan deprecation markers from ALL schema levels (not just `rules/checks`):
226	     - `rules/sidecars` â€” field-level `deprecated` annotations
227	     - `rules/checks` â€” validator check rules
228	     - `objects/metadata` â€” description-text deprecation markers
229	     - `objects/enums` â€” deprecated enum values
230	     - `objects/columns` â€” column-level deprecations
231	     - `objects/suffixes` â€” deprecated suffixes
232	   - Determine dataset's current version (from `dataset_description.json`)
233	   - Determine target version (default: current released 1.x; or `--to`)
234	   - Compute applicable rules (between source and target versions), filtered by level/id
235	4. Implement transformation handlers â€” **safe level** (auto-applied by default):
236	   - **Metadata field rename**: `BasedOn` â†’ `Sources`, `RawSources` â†’ `Sources`
237	   - **Value format changes**: relative paths â†’ BIDS URIs in `IntendedFor`, `AssociatedEmptyRoom`, `Sources`
238	   - **DOI format**: bare DOIs â†’ URI format in `DatasetDOI`
239	   - **Suffix deprecations**: `_phase` â†’ `_part-phase_bold` (delegates to `rename`)
240	   - **Enum value renames**: `ElektaNeuromag` â†’ `NeuromagElektaMEGIN`, `KitYokogawa` â†’ `YokogawaKIT`
241	   - **Cross-file moves**: `ScanDate` â†’ `acq_time` in `_scans.tsv`
242	   - **Conditional field rename**: `AcquisitionDuration` â†’ `FrameAcquisitionDuration` (only when `VolumeTiming` present) (FR-026)
243	   - **TSV column value**: `"89+"` string â†’ numeric `89` in `participants.tsv` `age` column, unit-aware (FR-027)
244	5. Implement transformation handlers â€” **advisory level** (opt-in via `--level=advisory`):
245	   - **Field removal**: `DCOffsetCorrection` deprecated in iEEG (FR-031)
246	   - **Field removal**: `HardcopyDeviceSoftwareVersion` deprecated in MRI (FR-032)
247	   - **HIPAA age cap**: numeric values > 89 â†’ `89` in `age` column (rule id: `age_cap_89`)
248	   - **Conditional field rename (ambiguous)**: `AcquisitionDuration` without `VolumeTiming` â€” prompt user
249	6. Implement transformation handlers â€” **non-auto-fixable** (report only):
250	   - **Ambiguous suffixes**: `T2star`, `FLASH`, `PD` â€” flag with replacement options
251	   - **Deprecated templates**: `fsaverage3-6`, `fsaveragesym`, `UNCInfant*V2x` â€” flag, no clear auto-replacement
252	7. **Age migration unit-awareness** (FR-027):
253	   - Check `participants.json` sidecar for `"Units"` on the `age` column definition
254	   - If units are non-year (months, days, etc.), skip the 89-year threshold rules and warn
255	   - TODO: upstream resolution needed on column unit override mechanism (bids-standard/bids-specification#1633)
256	8. Implement `cli/migrate.py`:
257	   - `--to VERSION`, `--level LEVEL`, `--mode MODE`, `--rule-id ID`, `--exclude-rule ID`
258	   - `--dry-run`, `--json`
259	   - Report: per-file changes with rule references, grouped by level
260	9. Tests:
261	   - Unit tests for each transformation type
262	   - Integration tests with crafted datasets containing known deprecations
263	   - `bids-examples` sweep: find datasets with older `BIDSVersion`, migrate, validate
264	   - Test `--level` and `--mode` filtering
265	
266	**Dependencies**: Phase 2 complete (uses rename for suffix changes)
267	
268	### Phase 3b: Schema Deprecation Audit (FR-033, FR-034)
269	
270	**Goal**: Automated coverage tracking to ensure all schema-declared deprecations have corresponding migration rules.
271	
272	**Steps**:
273	1. **Audit function** in `migrate.py`:
274	   - Scan all deprecation markers in the loaded `bidsschematools` schema across all levels
275	   - Compare against registered `MigrationRule` entries
276	   - Return list of unimplemented deprecations with schema location
277	   - Recommend: upgrade `bidsschematools` if not latest, or file an issue
278	2. **CLI subcommand**: `bids-utils migrate --audit`
279	   - Report: implemented vs missing migration rules, schema version
280	3. **Test**: `test_migration_coverage_vs_schema` â€” fails when new schema deprecations appear without corresponding rules
281	4. **GitHub issue template** (FR-034): `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/missing-migration-rule.yml`
282	   - Fields: schema version, deprecation location, affected field/suffix/enum, expected behavior
283	   - Labels: `migration`, `schema-gap`
284	
285	**Dependencies**: Phase 3 complete (needs the migration registry to audit against)
286	
287	### Phase 3c: BIDS URI Fixup Helper (FR-025)
288	
289	**Goal**: Generic helper that updates `bids:` URIs when files are renamed, reusable across all operations.
290	
291	**Steps**:
292	1. **Library function** in new `_bids_uri.py`:
293	   - `update_bids_uris(dataset, old_to_new: dict[Path, Path], dry_run) -> list[Change]`
294	   - Scan JSON metadata fields known to contain BIDS URIs: `IntendedFor`, `AssociatedEmptyRoom`, `Sources`, `DerivedFrom`
295	   - Match `bids::` scheme URIs against the oldâ†’new mapping
296	   - Update matched URIs
297	2. **Wire into rename/migrate/subject-rename/session-rename**: call after file renames
298	3. **Deferred**: cross-dataset URIs (`bids:ds001::...`), partial path matches
299	4. Tests: unit tests with crafted JSON sidecars containing BIDS URIs
300	
301	**Dependencies**: Phase 1 complete (needs `_io.py` for JSON read/write)
302	
303	### Phase 4: Migration â€” BIDS 2.0 (Story 3 â€” P1)
304	
305	**Goal**: `bids-utils migrate --to 2.0` handles 2.0 breaking changes.
306	
307	**Steps**:
308	1. **Concrete 2.0 rule** (FR-028, bids-standard/bids-2-devel#14):
309	   - Rename `participants.tsv` â†’ `subjects.tsv` (file rename via VCS)
310	   - Rename `participants.json` â†’ `subjects.json` (file rename via VCS)
311	   - Rename `participant_id` column â†’ `subject_id` in the renamed file
312	   - Update `BIDSVersion` in `dataset_description.json`
313	   - Update BIDS URIs referencing the old file via FR-025 helper
314	2. Extend migration rule engine for additional 2.0-specific transformations:
315	   - Entity renames (TBD from schema)
316	   - Structural reorganization (TBD from schema)
317	   - Metadata key changes (TBD from schema)
318	3. Ensure cumulative application: 1.x deprecations applied first, then 2.0 changes
319	4. Handle ambiguities: flag items requiring human judgment, skip with clear reporting
320	5. Tests:
321	   - Unit tests for participantsâ†’subjects rename
322	   - Integration tests against 2.0-dev schema
323	   - Validate migrated datasets against 2.0 validator schema
324	
325	**Dependencies**: Phase 3 + 3c complete
326	**Note**: Additional 2.0 transformations depend on BIDS 2.0 schema stabilization. This phase will iterate as the schema evolves.
327	
328	### Phase 5: Subject & Session Operations (Stories 4, 5 â€” P2)
329	
330	**Goal**: `bids-utils subject-rename` and `bids-utils session-rename` working.
331	
332	**Steps**:
333	1. **Subject rename** (`subject.py`):
334	   - Rename subject directory
335	   - Rename all files within (compose on `rename`)
336	   - Update `participants.tsv`
337	   - Update all `_scans.tsv` files
338	   - Optionally process `sourcedata/`, `.heudiconv/`, `derivatives/`
339	2. **Session rename** (`session.py`):
340	   - Similar to subject rename but for session entity
341	   - Special case: move-into-session (`'' â†’ ses-01`)
342	3. CLI wrappers with standard options
343	4. Tests:
344	   - bids-examples sweep for both operations
345	   - Edge cases: sourcedata, derivatives, git-annex
346	
347	**Dependencies**: Phase 2 complete
348	
349	### Phase 6: Metadata Operations (Story 6 â€” P2)
350	
351	**Goal**: `bids-utils metadata {aggregate,segregate,audit}` working.
352	
353	**Prior art**: IP-freely (@Lestropie) implements a graph-based relational model with bidirectional m4d/d4m mappings and ruleset-based inheritance behaviors. Key learnings: three inheritance behaviors (merge for `.json`, nearest for `.bval`/`.bvec`, forbidden for `.tsv`), parameterized rulesets, applicability rules (ancestor directory + entity subset matching + suffix matching). bids-utils should adopt the m4d/d4m pattern and add schema integration.
354	
355	**Steps**:
356	1. **Aggregate** (`metadata.py`):
357	   - Walk the inheritance hierarchy bottom-up
358	   - Identify common key-value pairs across all files at a level
359	   - Hoist common pairs to parent-level sidecar
360	   - Handle missing files correctly (do NOT aggregate if any file is absent)
361	   - Support scoped operation (per-subject, per-session)
362	   - Support `--mode copy|move`
363	2. **Segregate**: Push metadata down to leaf level (inverse of aggregate)
364	3. **Audit**: Report metadata values that are neither fully unique nor fully equivalent
365	4. CLI wrappers
366	5. Tests:
367	   - Verify resolved metadata is unchanged after aggregate + segregate round-trip
368	   - bids-examples sweep
369	
370	**Dependencies**: Phase 1 complete (independent of rename/migrate)
371	
372	### Phase 7: Remove & Merge/Split (Stories 7, 8, 9, 10 â€” P3)
373	
374	**Goal**: Lower-priority operations.
375	
376	**Steps**:
377	1. **Remove subject/session** (`subject.py`): Delete directory tree, update participants/scans
378	2. **Remove run** (`run.py`): Delete run files, optionally reindex subsequent runs
379	3. **Merge** (`merge.py`): Combine datasets, handle conflicts, session placement
380	4. **Split** (`split.py`): Extract subset by suffix/datatype
381	5. CLI wrappers and tests
382	
383	**Dependencies**: Phases 2, 5 complete
384	
385	## Key Design Decisions
386	
387	### 1. CLI Framework: Click
388	
389	**Decision**: Use `click` for CLI.
390	**Why**: Mature, well-documented, supports subcommands naturally, good testing support via `CliRunner`. The alternative (`argparse`) requires more boilerplate for subcommand groups.
391	
392	### 2. No PyBIDS Dependency
393	
394	**Decision**: Core operations use `bidsschematools` directly, not PyBIDS.
395	**Why**: Per constitution â€” PyBIDS brings considerable transitive complexity. Core operations (rename, migrate, metadata) can be implemented with just `bidsschematools` + filesystem ops.
396	
397	### 3. Entity Parsing: Custom, Schema-Driven
398	
399	**Decision**: Parse BIDS filenames using entity definitions from the schema.
400	**Why**: Hardcoded entity lists would violate Principle II. The schema defines entity ordering and allowed values per datatype.
401	
402	### 4. Atomic Operations via VCS
403	
404	**Decision**: When VCS is present, each command is a single atomic operation (single commit).
405	**Why**: Makes operations reversible via `git revert`. When no VCS, operations are best-effort with clear reporting.
406	
407	### 5. `_scans.tsv` and `participants.tsv` Updates Are Automatic
408	
409	**Decision**: Every operation that renames/removes files automatically updates these files.
410	**Why**: Leaving stale references breaks dataset validity (Principle I).
411	
412	## Risk Assessment
413	
414	| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
415	|------|-----------|--------|------------|
416	| `bidsschematools` API changes | Medium | High | Pin to compatible version range; abstract behind `_schema.py` |
417	| BIDS 2.0 schema not finalized | High | Medium | Phase 4 is designed to iterate; 1.x migration is independently useful. participantsâ†’subjects (issue #14) is concrete and implementable now. |
418	| git-annex edge cases | Medium | Medium | Test with locked/unlocked files; handle gracefully when content unavailable |
419	| Large dataset performance | Low | Medium | Profile early; use lazy evaluation; batch file operations |
420	| Cross-platform path handling | Medium | Low | Use `pathlib` throughout; test on Windows CI |
421	| Schema deprecation drift | Medium | Medium | FR-033 audit test fails on new deprecations; CI catches gaps automatically |
422	| Age column units ambiguity | Medium | Low | Unit-aware migration; skip non-year units with warning; upstream issue bids-specification#1633 tracks resolution |
423	| Advisory migration data loss | Low | High | Advisory rules require opt-in (`--level=advisory`); interactive mode prompts per-finding |
424	
425	## Complexity Tracking
426	
427	No constitution violations identified. The plan follows all 11 principles:
428	- Single project structure (Principle IX)
429	- All BIDS knowledge from schema (Principle II)
430	- Library functions before CLI (Principle III)
431	- TDD with bids-examples (Principle V)
432

file.content:
# Implementation Plan: bids-utils â€” Core Library & CLI

**Branch**: `000-initial-design` | **Date**: 2026-04-03 | **Spec**: [000-initial-design.md](../000-initial-design.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `.specify/specs/000-initial-design.md`

## Summary

Build `bids-utils`, a Python library and CLI for manipulating BIDS datasets. Core operations: file renaming (with sidecar/scans tracking), schema-driven migration (1.x deprecations + 2.0), metadata aggregation/segregation, subject/session renaming, and dataset merge/split. All operations are schema-driven via `bidsschematools`, VCS-aware, and validated against `bids-examples`.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.10+ (per spec assumptions)
**Primary Dependencies**: `bidsschematools` (schema access), `click` (CLI framework)
**Optional Dependencies**: `bids-validator-deno` (testing), `bids2table` (dataset querying, if needed)
**Storage**: Filesystem (BIDS datasets are directory trees)
**Testing**: `pytest` orchestrated by `tox` (with `tox-uv`)
**Target Platform**: Linux, macOS, Windows (cross-platform filesystem operations)
**Project Type**: Library + CLI
**Performance Goals**: O(n) in affected files, not O(nÂ˛) in total dataset size. Usable on 1000-subject datasets.
**Constraints**: Must not corrupt valid BIDS datasets. Must support git/git-annex/DataLad workflows.
**Scale/Scope**: Single-developer start, community contributions expected. ~10 CLI commands at maturity.

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| I. Do No Harm | PASS | Every operation validates affected entities; `--dry-run` mandatory; atomic operations |
| II. Schema-Driven | PASS | All BIDS knowledge from `bidsschematools`; multi-version support; FR-033 audit ensures schema/rule sync across all 6 deprecation levels |
| III. Library-First | PASS | Every CLI command maps to a public library function |
| IV. CLI Excellence | PASS | `--dry-run`, `--json`, `-v`/`-q`, meaningful exit codes for every command |
| V. Test-First | PASS | TDD enforced; `bids-examples` sweep testing; randomized testing for coverage |
| VI. Performance | PASS | Lazy evaluation; no full-dataset loading for single-entity operations |
| VII. VCS Awareness | PASS | Auto-detect git/git-annex/DataLad; use VCS primitives when present |
| VIII. Observability | PASS | Structured logging; JSON change manifests; dry-run parity |
| IX. Simplicity | PASS | Flat module structure; composition over monoliths; YAGNI |
| X. Versioning | PASS | SemVer; automated releases via intuit/auto |
| XI. DRY | PASS | Duplication detection in CI (pylint + jscpd) |

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
.specify/specs/000-initial-design/
â”śâ”€â”€ plan.md              # This file
â”śâ”€â”€ research.md          # Prior art & ecosystem analysis
â”śâ”€â”€ data-model.md        # Core data model design
â”śâ”€â”€ quickstart.md        # Getting started guide
â”śâ”€â”€ contracts/           # Interface contracts
â””â”€â”€ tasks.md             # Implementation tasks (via /speckit.tasks)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
pyproject.toml           # Single source of truth for deps, metadata, build
tox.ini                  # Test orchestration (pytest, lint, type, duplication)
mkdocs.yml               # Documentation site config

src/bids_utils/
â”śâ”€â”€ __init__.py          # Package root, version
â”śâ”€â”€ _types.py            # Shared type definitions (PathLike, Entity, etc.)
â”śâ”€â”€ _vcs.py              # VCS detection and operations (git mv, git annex, datalad)
â”śâ”€â”€ _schema.py           # Schema loading and querying helpers (wraps bidsschematools)
â”śâ”€â”€ _io.py               # Content-aware file I/O (annexed content policy enforcement)
â”śâ”€â”€ _tsv.py              # Shared TSV read/write utilities (used by _scans.py, _participants.py)
â”śâ”€â”€ _scans.py            # _scans.tsv read/write/update operations
â”śâ”€â”€ _participants.py     # participants.tsv read/write/update operations
â”śâ”€â”€ _sidecars.py         # Sidecar discovery (find all associated files for a BIDS file)
â”śâ”€â”€ _dataset.py          # Dataset-level operations (find root, read dataset_description)
â”śâ”€â”€ rename.py            # File rename: core operation (Story 1)
â”śâ”€â”€ migrate.py           # Schema-driven migration (Stories 2, 3)
â”śâ”€â”€ metadata.py          # Metadata aggregate/segregate/audit (Story 6)
â”śâ”€â”€ subject.py           # Subject rename/remove (Stories 4, 7)
â”śâ”€â”€ session.py           # Session rename/move-into-session (Story 5)
â”śâ”€â”€ merge.py             # Dataset merge (Story 9)
â”śâ”€â”€ split.py             # Dataset split (Story 10)
â”śâ”€â”€ run.py               # Run remove with reindexing (Story 8)
â””â”€â”€ cli/
    â”śâ”€â”€ __init__.py      # CLI entry point (click group)
    â”śâ”€â”€ _common.py       # Shared CLI options (--dry-run, --json, -v/-q, --force)
    â”śâ”€â”€ rename.py         # bids-utils rename
    â”śâ”€â”€ migrate.py        # bids-utils migrate
    â”śâ”€â”€ metadata.py       # bids-utils metadata {aggregate,segregate,audit}
    â”śâ”€â”€ subject.py        # bids-utils subject-rename, bids-utils remove
    â”śâ”€â”€ session.py        # bids-utils session-rename
    â”śâ”€â”€ merge.py          # bids-utils merge
    â”śâ”€â”€ split.py          # bids-utils split
    â””â”€â”€ run.py            # bids-utils remove-run

tests/
â”śâ”€â”€ conftest.py          # Shared fixtures (tmp BIDS datasets, bids-examples access)
â”śâ”€â”€ test_rename.py       # Unit + integration tests for rename
â”śâ”€â”€ test_migrate.py      # Migration tests (multi-version)
â”śâ”€â”€ test_metadata.py     # Metadata manipulation tests
â”śâ”€â”€ test_subject.py      # Subject operations tests
â”śâ”€â”€ test_session.py      # Session operations tests
â”śâ”€â”€ test_merge.py        # Merge tests
â”śâ”€â”€ test_split.py        # Split tests
â”śâ”€â”€ test_run.py          # Run removal tests
â”śâ”€â”€ test_io.py           # Content-aware I/O tests (annexed modes)
â”śâ”€â”€ test_vcs.py          # VCS integration tests
â”śâ”€â”€ test_cli.py          # CLI smoke tests
â”śâ”€â”€ test_cli_common.py   # Tests for shared CLI options/decorators
â”śâ”€â”€ test_tsv.py          # Tests for shared TSV utilities
â””â”€â”€ integration/
    â””â”€â”€ test_bids_examples.py  # Sweep tests against bids-examples
```

**Structure Decision**: Single-project layout with `src/` layout (PEP 517/518 compliant). Library modules at `src/bids_utils/`, CLI as a subpackage. Private modules prefixed with `_` for internal utilities. This is the simplest structure that supports the library-first + CLI wrapper architecture.

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 0: Project Scaffolding (Foundation)

**Goal**: Working project skeleton with CI, linting, type checking, and an empty CLI.

**Steps**:
1. Initialize project using copier-astral template (or manual setup with uv)
2. Configure `pyproject.toml` with dependency layers (test/devel/ci)
3. Configure `tox.ini` with envs: py310-py314, lint, type, duplication
4. Set up GitHub Actions workflow (invoke tox via tox-gh-actions)
5. Configure mkdocs with basic structure
6. Create `src/bids_utils/__init__.py` with version
7. Create `src/bids_utils/cli/__init__.py` with click group entry point
8. Set up intuit/auto for automated releases
9. Add `bids-examples` as a git submodule for testing
10. Verify: `tox` passes, `bids-utils --help` works, CI green

**Dependencies**: None (first phase)

### Phase 1: Core Infrastructure (Private Modules)

**Goal**: Build the shared utilities that all commands depend on.

**Steps** (implement in this order, each with tests first):

1. **`_types.py`**: Type definitions â€” `BIDSPath`, `Entity` (key-value pair), `EntitySet`, path-like protocols
2. **`_dataset.py`**: Find dataset root (walk up to `dataset_description.json`), read `BIDSVersion`, detect BIDS validity basics
3. **`_schema.py`**: Wrap `bidsschematools.schema.load_schema()` â€” load by version, query entities, query suffixes, query sidecar extensions, query deprecation rules
4. **`_vcs.py`**: Detect VCS type (none, git, git-annex, datalad). Provide `move()`, `remove()`, `commit()` that dispatch to the right backend. Handle dirty-tree detection.
5. **`_sidecars.py`**: Given a BIDS file path, find all associated sidecars by replacing extension with each known sidecar extension (from schema). Handle the case where sidecar might be at a higher level (inheritance).
6. **`_scans.py`**: Read/write `_scans.tsv`. Find the scans file for a given file. Update/remove entries by filename.
7. **`_participants.py`**: Read/write `participants.tsv`. Add/remove/rename subject entries.

**Dependencies**: Phase 0 complete

### Phase 1b: Annexed Content Handling (FR-022)

**Goal**: All file reads work correctly on git-annex/DataLad datasets via a `--annexed` policy option.

**Steps**:
1. **Foundation types**: Add `AnnexedMode` enum and `ContentNotAvailableError` to `_types.py`. Add `annexed_mode` field to `BIDSDataset`.
2. **VCS protocol extension**: Extend `VCSBackend` protocol with four new methods:
   - `has_content(path) -> bool` / `get_content(paths)` â€” for reads
   - `unlock(paths)` / `add(paths)` â€” for writes (unlock locked annexed files before modification, re-annex after)
   - Implementations: `NoVCS`/`Git` â†’ trivial (always True, no-op for unlock, `git add` for add); `GitAnnex` â†’ check symlink target, `git annex get/unlock/add`; `DataLad` â†’ `datalad get/unlock`, `git annex add`.
3. **Content-aware I/O** (`_io.py`):
   - `ensure_content(path, vcs, mode)` â€” enforces `--annexed` policy for reads
   - `ensure_writable(path, vcs)` â€” unlocks locked annexed files before writes (always, regardless of `--annexed` mode)
   - `mark_modified(paths, vcs)` â€” calls `vcs.add()` after writes to re-annex files
   - `read_json(path, vcs, mode)` / `write_json(path, data, vcs)` â€” content-aware JSON I/O
4. **Wire through existing code**: Update `_tsv.read_tsv`/`write_tsv` with optional VCS/mode params. Update all callers. Replace inline JSON reads/writes in `metadata.py` (~6 read + ~3 write sites) and `migrate.py` (~11 read + ~6 write sites) with `_io` helpers.
5. **CLI wiring**: Add `--annexed` to Click group with `envvar="BIDS_UTILS_ANNEXED"`. `load_dataset()` sets `annexed_mode` on the returned `BIDSDataset`.
6. **Tests**: Mock VCS tests for all four modes. Unlock/add lifecycle tests. Integration test with real git-annex repo (locked files, content present/absent).

**Dependencies**: Phase 1 complete. Can be done at any point after Phase 1, but should be done before real-world usage on annexed datasets.

### Phase 1c: Symlink Safety & Dry-Run Detail (FR-003, FR-023, FR-024)

**Goal**: Fix the `is_file()` symlink bug that silently skips annexed data files during rename operations. Enhance `--dry-run` to show per-file detail. Add annex operation logging.

**Steps**:
1. **Symlink bug fix (T092)**: Audit all `is_file()` calls used for file iteration. Replace with `not path.is_dir()` in `session.py`, `subject.py`, `run.py`, `split.py`, `merge.py`, `_sidecars.py`, `migrate.py`. Keep `is_file()` where checking for file existence (not iteration).
2. **Annex test fixture (T093)**: `tmp_annex_dataset` in conftest.py â€” git-annex repo with locked symlinks alongside regular files.
3. **Regression tests (T094)**: Session/subject/file rename on annexed dataset â€” verify all files including symlinks are renamed.
4. **Dry-run detail (T095-T096)**: `--dry-run=overview|detailed`. Update `common_options`, ensure all library functions populate per-file `Change` entries. `output_result` renders overview vs detailed.
5. **Annex logging (T097)**: INFO-level logging for get/unlock/add operations in `_io.py`.
6. **Tests (T098)**: Verify `--dry-run=detailed` output.

**Dependencies**: Phase 1b complete. BLOCKS real-world usage on annexed datasets.

### Phase 2: File Rename (Story 1 â€” P1)

**Goal**: `bids-utils rename` working end-to-end with full test coverage.

**Steps**:
1. Implement `rename.py` library function:
   - Parse source file path into entities
   - Accept entity overrides (e.g., `--set task=nback`)
   - Compute new filename from modified entities
   - Discover all sidecars for the source file
   - Check for conflicts (target already exists)
   - Execute renames (filesystem or VCS)
   - Update `_scans.tsv` if applicable
2. Implement `cli/rename.py`:
   - Wire up arguments, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `-v`/`-q`
   - Human-readable and JSON output modes
3. Tests:
   - Unit tests for entity parsing, filename construction
   - Integration tests with tmp BIDS datasets
   - `bids-examples` sweep: rename a random file, validate dataset

**Dependencies**: Phase 1 complete

### Phase 3: Migration â€” 1.x Deprecations (Story 2 â€” P1)

**Goal**: `bids-utils migrate` handles all known 1.x deprecations with tiered migration levels.

**Prior art**: PR #2282's decorator-based migration registry pattern is directly reusable. It implements `@registry.register(name="...", version="1.10.0", description="...")` with dry-run support and JSON-safe operations. Currently handles 3 migrations; bids-utils must extend to cover all 1.x deprecations.

**Steps**:
1. **Extend MigrationRule schema** (FR-029):
   - Add `level` field to `MigrationRule`: `safe` | `advisory` | `non-auto-fixable`
   - Add `condition` field for contextual rules (e.g., `AcquisitionDuration` requires `VolumeTiming`)
   - This formalizes the migration record schema (analogous to validator schemas, cf. dandi-cli)
2. **Implement CLI filtering** (FR-030):
   - `--level=safe` (default), `--level=advisory`, `--level=all`
   - `--rule-id=ID` / `--exclude-rule=ID` for per-rule control
   - `--mode=auto` (default: interactive when PTY, non-interactive otherwise), `--mode=non-interactive`, `--mode=interactive`
   - Library API: `level`, `mode`, `rule_ids`, `exclude_rules` parameters on `migrate_dataset()`
3. Implement migration rule engine in `migrate.py`:
   - Scan deprecation markers from ALL schema levels (not just `rules/checks`):
     - `rules/sidecars` â€” field-level `deprecated` annotations
     - `rules/checks` â€” validator check rules
     - `objects/metadata` â€” description-text deprecation markers
     - `objects/enums` â€” deprecated enum values
     - `objects/columns` â€” column-level deprecations
     - `objects/suffixes` â€” deprecated suffixes
   - Determine dataset's current version (from `dataset_description.json`)
   - Determine target version (default: current released 1.x; or `--to`)
   - Compute applicable rules (between source and target versions), filtered by level/id
4. Implement transformation handlers â€” **safe level** (auto-applied by default):
   - **Metadata field rename**: `BasedOn` â†’ `Sources`, `RawSources` â†’ `Sources`
   - **Value format changes**: relative paths â†’ BIDS URIs in `IntendedFor`, `AssociatedEmptyRoom`, `Sources`
   - **DOI format**: bare DOIs â†’ URI format in `DatasetDOI`
   - **Suffix deprecations**: `_phase` â†’ `_part-phase_bold` (delegates to `rename`)
   - **Enum value renames**: `ElektaNeuromag` â†’ `NeuromagElektaMEGIN`, `KitYokogawa` â†’ `YokogawaKIT`
   - **Cross-file moves**: `ScanDate` â†’ `acq_time` in `_scans.tsv`
   - **Conditional field rename**: `AcquisitionDuration` â†’ `FrameAcquisitionDuration` (only when `VolumeTiming` present) (FR-026)
   - **TSV column value**: `"89+"` string â†’ numeric `89` in `participants.tsv` `age` column, unit-aware (FR-027)
5. Implement transformation handlers â€” **advisory level** (opt-in via `--level=advisory`):
   - **Field removal**: `DCOffsetCorrection` deprecated in iEEG (FR-031)
   - **Field removal**: `HardcopyDeviceSoftwareVersion` deprecated in MRI (FR-032)
   - **HIPAA age cap**: numeric values > 89 â†’ `89` in `age` column (rule id: `age_cap_89`)
   - **Conditional field rename (ambiguous)**: `AcquisitionDuration` without `VolumeTiming` â€” prompt user
6. Implement transformation handlers â€” **non-auto-fixable** (report only):
   - **Ambiguous suffixes**: `T2star`, `FLASH`, `PD` â€” flag with replacement options
   - **Deprecated templates**: `fsaverage3-6`, `fsaveragesym`, `UNCInfant*V2x` â€” flag, no clear auto-replacement
7. **Age migration unit-awareness** (FR-027):
   - Check `participants.json` sidecar for `"Units"` on the `age` column definition
   - If units are non-year (months, days, etc.), skip the 89-year threshold rules and warn
   - TODO: upstream resolution needed on column unit override mechanism (bids-standard/bids-specification#1633)
8. Implement `cli/migrate.py`:
   - `--to VERSION`, `--level LEVEL`, `--mode MODE`, `--rule-id ID`, `--exclude-rule ID`
   - `--dry-run`, `--json`
   - Report: per-file changes with rule references, grouped by level
9. Tests:
   - Unit tests for each transformation type
   - Integration tests with crafted datasets containing known deprecations
   - `bids-examples` sweep: find datasets with older `BIDSVersion`, migrate, validate
   - Test `--level` and `--mode` filtering

**Dependencies**: Phase 2 complete (uses rename for suffix changes)

### Phase 3b: Schema Deprecation Audit (FR-033, FR-034)

**Goal**: Automated coverage tracking to ensure all schema-declared deprecations have corresponding migration rules.

**Steps**:
1. **Audit function** in `migrate.py`:
   - Scan all deprecation markers in the loaded `bidsschematools` schema across all levels
   - Compare against registered `MigrationRule` entries
   - Return list of unimplemented deprecations with schema location
   - Recommend: upgrade `bidsschematools` if not latest, or file an issue
2. **CLI subcommand**: `bids-utils migrate --audit`
   - Report: implemented vs missing migration rules, schema version
3. **Test**: `test_migration_coverage_vs_schema` â€” fails when new schema deprecations appear without corresponding rules
4. **GitHub issue template** (FR-034): `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/missing-migration-rule.yml`
   - Fields: schema version, deprecation location, affected field/suffix/enum, expected behavior
   - Labels: `migration`, `schema-gap`

**Dependencies**: Phase 3 complete (needs the migration registry to audit against)

### Phase 3c: BIDS URI Fixup Helper (FR-025)

**Goal**: Generic helper that updates `bids:` URIs when files are renamed, reusable across all operations.

**Steps**:
1. **Library function** in new `_bids_uri.py`:
   - `update_bids_uris(dataset, old_to_new: dict[Path, Path], dry_run) -> list[Change]`
   - Scan JSON metadata fields known to contain BIDS URIs: `IntendedFor`, `AssociatedEmptyRoom`, `Sources`, `DerivedFrom`
   - Match `bids::` scheme URIs against the oldâ†’new mapping
   - Update matched URIs
2. **Wire into rename/migrate/subject-rename/session-rename**: call after file renames
3. **Deferred**: cross-dataset URIs (`bids:ds001::...`), partial path matches
4. Tests: unit tests with crafted JSON sidecars containing BIDS URIs

**Dependencies**: Phase 1 complete (needs `_io.py` for JSON read/write)

### Phase 4: Migration â€” BIDS 2.0 (Story 3 â€” P1)

**Goal**: `bids-utils migrate --to 2.0` handles 2.0 breaking changes.

**Steps**:
1. **Concrete 2.0 rule** (FR-028, bids-standard/bids-2-devel#14):
   - Rename `participants.tsv` â†’ `subjects.tsv` (file rename via VCS)
   - Rename `participants.json` â†’ `subjects.json` (file rename via VCS)
   - Rename `participant_id` column â†’ `subject_id` in the renamed file
   - Update `BIDSVersion` in `dataset_description.json`
   - Update BIDS URIs referencing the old file via FR-025 helper
2. Extend migration rule engine for additional 2.0-specific transformations:
   - Entity renames (TBD from schema)
   - Structural reorganization (TBD from schema)
   - Metadata key changes (TBD from schema)
3. Ensure cumulative application: 1.x deprecations applied first, then 2.0 changes
4. Handle ambiguities: flag items requiring human judgment, skip with clear reporting
5. Tests:
   - Unit tests for participantsâ†’subjects rename
   - Integration tests against 2.0-dev schema
   - Validate migrated datasets against 2.0 validator schema

**Dependencies**: Phase 3 + 3c complete
**Note**: Additional 2.0 transformations depend on BIDS 2.0 schema stabilization. This phase will iterate as the schema evolves.

### Phase 5: Subject & Session Operations (Stories 4, 5 â€” P2)

**Goal**: `bids-utils subject-rename` and `bids-utils session-rename` working.

**Steps**:
1. **Subject rename** (`subject.py`):
   - Rename subject directory
   - Rename all files within (compose on `rename`)
   - Update `participants.tsv`
   - Update all `_scans.tsv` files
   - Optionally process `sourcedata/`, `.heudiconv/`, `derivatives/`
2. **Session rename** (`session.py`):
   - Similar to subject rename but for session entity
   - Special case: move-into-session (`'' â†’ ses-01`)
3. CLI wrappers with standard options
4. Tests:
   - bids-examples sweep for both operations
   - Edge cases: sourcedata, derivatives, git-annex

**Dependencies**: Phase 2 complete

### Phase 6: Metadata Operations (Story 6 â€” P2)

**Goal**: `bids-utils metadata {aggregate,segregate,audit}` working.

**Prior art**: IP-freely (@Lestropie) implements a graph-based relational model with bidirectional m4d/d4m mappings and ruleset-based inheritance behaviors. Key learnings: three inheritance behaviors (merge for `.json`, nearest for `.bval`/`.bvec`, forbidden for `.tsv`), parameterized rulesets, applicability rules (ancestor directory + entity subset matching + suffix matching). bids-utils should adopt the m4d/d4m pattern and add schema integration.

**Steps**:
1. **Aggregate** (`metadata.py`):
   - Walk the inheritance hierarchy bottom-up
   - Identify common key-value pairs across all files at a level
   - Hoist common pairs to parent-level sidecar
   - Handle missing files correctly (do NOT aggregate if any file is absent)
   - Support scoped operation (per-subject, per-session)
   - Support `--mode copy|move`
2. **Segregate**: Push metadata down to leaf level (inverse of aggregate)
3. **Audit**: Report metadata values that are neither fully unique nor fully equivalent
4. CLI wrappers
5. Tests:
   - Verify resolved metadata is unchanged after aggregate + segregate round-trip
   - bids-examples sweep

**Dependencies**: Phase 1 complete (independent of rename/migrate)

### Phase 7: Remove & Merge/Split (Stories 7, 8, 9, 10 â€” P3)

**Goal**: Lower-priority operations.

**Steps**:
1. **Remove subject/session** (`subject.py`): Delete directory tree, update participants/scans
2. **Remove run** (`run.py`): Delete run files, optionally reindex subsequent runs
3. **Merge** (`merge.py`): Combine datasets, handle conflicts, session placement
4. **Split** (`split.py`): Extract subset by suffix/datatype
5. CLI wrappers and tests

**Dependencies**: Phases 2, 5 complete

## Key Design Decisions

### 1. CLI Framework: Click

**Decision**: Use `click` for CLI.
**Why**: Mature, well-documented, supports subcommands naturally, good testing support via `CliRunner`. The alternative (`argparse`) requires more boilerplate for subcommand groups.

### 2. No PyBIDS Dependency

**Decision**: Core operations use `bidsschematools` directly, not PyBIDS.
**Why**: Per constitution â€” PyBIDS brings considerable transitive complexity. Core operations (rename, migrate, metadata) can be implemented with just `bidsschematools` + filesystem ops.

### 3. Entity Parsing: Custom, Schema-Driven

**Decision**: Parse BIDS filenames using entity definitions from the schema.
**Why**: Hardcoded entity lists would violate Principle II. The schema defines entity ordering and allowed values per datatype.

### 4. Atomic Operations via VCS

**Decision**: When VCS is present, each command is a single atomic operation (single commit).
**Why**: Makes operations reversible via `git revert`. When no VCS, operations are best-effort with clear reporting.

### 5. `_scans.tsv` and `participants.tsv` Updates Are Automatic

**Decision**: Every operation that renames/removes files automatically updates these files.
**Why**: Leaving stale references breaks dataset validity (Principle I).

## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|--------|------------|
| `bidsschematools` API changes | Medium | High | Pin to compatible version range; abstract behind `_schema.py` |
| BIDS 2.0 schema not finalized | High | Medium | Phase 4 is designed to iterate; 1.x migration is independently useful. participantsâ†’subjects (issue #14) is concrete and implementable now. |
| git-annex edge cases | Medium | Medium | Test with locked/unlocked files; handle gracefully when content unavailable |
| Large dataset performance | Low | Medium | Profile early; use lazy evaluation; batch file operations |
| Cross-platform path handling | Medium | Low | Use `pathlib` throughout; test on Windows CI |
| Schema deprecation drift | Medium | Medium | FR-033 audit test fails on new deprecations; CI catches gaps automatically |
| Age column units ambiguity | Medium | Low | Unit-aware migration; skip non-year units with warning; upstream issue bids-specification#1633 tracks resolution |
| Advisory migration data loss | Low | High | Advisory rules require opt-in (`--level=advisory`); interactive mode prompts per-finding |

## Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations identified. The plan follows all 11 principles:
- Single project structure (Principle IX)
- All BIDS knowledge from schema (Principle II)
- Library functions before CLI (Principle III)
- TDD with bids-examples (Principle V)

351	  - `participants.tsv` conflicts reported
352	  - Metadata conflicts handled with segregate/re-aggregate
353	- [X] T071 [US9] Implement `src/bids_utils/cli/merge.py`: `bids-utils merge` click command
354	- [X] T072 [US9] Write `bids-examples` integration test: merge two datasets, validate
355	
356	**Checkpoint**: Dataset merge functional.
357	
358	---
359	
360	## Phase 10: User Story 10 â€” Split Datasets (Priority: P3)
361	
362	**Goal**: `bids-utils split` extracts subset of a dataset by suffix/datatype.
363	
364	### Implementation
365	
366	- [X] T073 [US10] Implement `src/bids_utils/split.py`: `split_dataset()` â€” extract files matching suffix/datatype filter, include required metadata, produce valid BIDS dataset
367	- [X] T074 [US10] Write tests for `split.py` in `tests/test_split.py`: split by suffix produces valid dataset with required metadata
368	- [X] T075 [US10] Implement `src/bids_utils/cli/split.py`: `bids-utils split` click command

374	## Phase 11: Shell Completion (FR-019, FR-020, FR-021)

376	**Purpose**: `bids-utils completion` subcommand with BIDS-aware completions.
377	
378	**Independent Test**: Run `bids-utils completion bash | source /dev/stdin`, verify tab-completion offers `sub-*`, `ses-*` directories and entity keys.
379	
380	### Implementation
381	
382	- [X] T083 [P] Implement `src/bids_utils/cli/completion.py`: `bids-utils completion [SHELL]` click command â€” auto-detect shell from `$SHELL`, output activation script to stdout. Supported: Bash, Zsh, Fish (Click 8.0+ built-in).
383	- [X] T084 Implement BIDS-aware custom completions: filesystem-derived items (`sub-*` directories, `ses-*` directories, BIDS file paths) and entity keys from schema (`task=`, `run=`, `acq=`). Uses `_dataset.py` for dataset root resolution (FR-020: honor `--dataset` or walk up from CWD to `dataset_description.json`).
384	- [X] T085 Write tests for completion in `tests/test_cli.py` or `tests/test_completion.py`: `bids-utils completion --help`, shell detection, activation script output for each shell, BIDS-aware completion produces expected items
385

389	
390	## Phase 1c: Symlink Safety & Dry-Run Detail (FR-003, FR-023, FR-024)
391	
392	**Purpose**: Fix critical git-annex symlink handling bug and enhance `--dry-run` to show per-file detail. These are blocking issues for real-world usage on annexed datasets.
393	
394	### Bug fix: `is_file()` skips annexed symlinks (FR-023)
395	
396	- [X] T092 Replace all bare `path.is_file()` calls used for file iteration with `not path.is_dir()` (or `path.is_file() or path.is_symlink()`) in: `session.py` (2 sites), `subject.py` (2 sites), `run.py` (2 sites), `split.py` (1 site), `merge.py` (1 site), `_sidecars.py` (1 site), `migrate.py` (1 site). Preserve `is_file()` where semantically correct (e.g., `_dataset.py` checking `dataset_description.json` existence, `_scans.py` checking `_scans.tsv` existence â€” these are never annexed).
397	- [X] T093 Add `tmp_annex_dataset` pytest fixture in `tests/conftest.py`: creates a git-annex repo with locked (symlinked) data files (`.nii.gz`) alongside regular git files (`.json`, `.tsv`). Requires `git annex` to be installed (mark tests `skipif` otherwise).
398	- [X] T094 Write regression tests using `tmp_annex_dataset` for session-rename, subject-rename, and rename â€” verify that ALL files (including annexed symlinks) are renamed correctly (SC-008). Test both with content present and content absent.
399	- [X] T124 [US4,US5] Implement `_is_bids_data_entry(path: Path) -> bool` helper in `src/bids_utils/_types.py` (FR-036). Returns `True` for: (a) regular files, (b) symlinks (annexed files per FR-023), (c) directories matching BIDS data directory patterns (`.ds`, `.zarr`, `.ome.zarr`). Replace all `not path.is_dir()` file iteration filters (introduced by T092) with `_is_bids_data_entry()` in: `session.py`, `subject.py`, `run.py`, `split.py`, `merge.py`, `_sidecars.py`, `migrate.py`. Add test with mock `.ds` directory â€” verify it is included in rename operations.
400	
401	### Enhanced dry-run (FR-003 update)
402	
403	- [X] T095 Change `--dry-run` / `-n` from a boolean flag to an optional-value option: `--dry-run` (or `--dry-run=overview`) for current summary behavior, `--dry-run=detailed` for per-file listing. Update `common_options` in `cli/_common.py`, `OperationResult`, and `output_result()`. Library functions already populate `result.changes` with per-file detail â€” the change is in how `output_result` renders them.
404	- [X] T096 Ensure all library functions populate `result.changes` with per-file detail (not just one summary `Change` per subject/session). Audit `session.py`, `subject.py`, `rename.py` â€” the rename function already does this; session/subject need to add per-file `Change` entries for individual file renames within the session/subject operation.
405	
406	### Annex operation logging (FR-024)
407	
408	- [X] T097 Add logging to `_io.py` for annex operations: log at INFO level when `ensure_content` fetches a file (`--annexed=get`), when `ensure_writable` unlocks, when `mark_modified` re-adds. In `--dry-run` mode, report which files would need content fetched. Wire through to CLI verbosity (`-v` enables DEBUG, default shows INFO, `-q` suppresses).
409	
410	### Tests
411	
412	- [X] T098 Write tests for `--dry-run=detailed` output: verify per-file change listing for session-rename, subject-rename, rename. Verify `--dry-run=overview` retains current behavior. Verify `--dry-run` without value defaults to overview.
413	
414	**Checkpoint**: `bids-utils --annexed=get session-rename --dry-run=detailed` shows every file that would be renamed/edited/fetched. Running without `--dry-run` on an annexed dataset correctly renames all files including symlinks.
415	
416	---
417	
418	## Phase 12: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns
419	
420	**Purpose**: Improvements that affect multiple user stories.
421	
422	- [ ] T076 [P] Documentation: populate `mkdocs` site with quickstart, API reference, CLI reference
423	- [ ] T077 [P] Add `--json` output mode tests for all commands (SC-005)
424	- [X] T078 Run full `bids-examples` sweep across all operations (SC-001, SC-008). **Two modes**: (a) plain git (current bids-examples), (b) git-annex mode â€” clone bids-examples, `git annex init`, force all files into annex (`git annex add .`), then run all operations. Both modes must produce equivalent results. Operations to test per dataset: rename (random file), session-rename (if multi-session), subject-rename, migrate --dry-run. Validate dataset remains valid after each operation.
425	- [ ] T079 [P] Test suite against multiple BIDS schema versions (1.8, 1.9, 2.0-dev) (SC-006)
426	- [ ] T080 [P] Performance profiling on a 1000-subject synthetic dataset (SC-003)
427	- [X] T081 Code cleanup: check for duplication (`tox -e duplication`), refactor
428	- [X] T082 Run `quickstart.md` validation â€” verify all documented commands work
429	
430	---
431	
432	## Dependencies & Execution Order
433	
434	### Phase Dependencies
435	
436	- **Phase 0 (Scaffolding)**: No dependencies â€” start immediately
437	- **Phase 1 (Infrastructure)**: Depends on Phase 0 â€” BLOCKS all user stories
438	- **Phase 1b (Annexed Content / FR-022)**: Depends on Phase 1. Can be done at any point but SHOULD be done before real-world usage on git-annex/DataLad datasets. Retroactively completes VCS integration from Phase 1.
439	- **Phase 1c (Symlink Safety & Dry-Run Detail / FR-003, FR-023, FR-024)**: Depends on Phase 1b. BLOCKS real-world usage on annexed datasets â€” the symlink bug causes silent data loss (files not renamed). Should be done immediately after Phase 1b.
440	- **Phase 2 (Rename / US1)**: Depends on Phase 1
441	- **Phase 3 (Migrate 1.x / US2)**: Depends on Phase 2 (uses rename for suffix changes)
442	- **Phase 3a (Migration Rule Schema / FR-029, FR-030)**: Depends on Phase 3 (extends existing migrate.py)
443	- **Phase 3b (Schema Audit / FR-033, FR-034)**: Depends on Phase 3a (needs level field and rule registry)
444	- **Phase 3c (BIDS URI Fixup / FR-025)**: Depends on Phase 1 (independent of migrate, but blocks Phase 4 concrete rules)
445	- **Phase 4 (Migrate 2.0 / US3)**: Depends on Phase 3a + Phase 3c (needs tiered levels and URI fixup for participantsâ†’subjects)
446	- **Phase 5 (Subject rename / US4)**: Depends on Phase 2
447	- **Phase 6 (Session rename / US5)**: Depends on Phase 2
448	- **Phase 7 (Metadata / US6)**: Depends on Phase 1 (independent of rename/migrate)
449	- **Phase 8 (Remove / US7-8)**: Depends on Phase 2
450	- **Phase 9 (Merge / US9)**: Depends on Phases 5, 6 (uses subject/session rename)
451	- **Phase 10 (Split / US10)**: Depends on Phase 1
452	- **Phase 11 (Completion / FR-019-021)**: Depends on Phase 1 (uses `_dataset.py`, `_schema.py`)
453	- **Phase 12 (Polish)**: Depends on all desired phases being complete
454	
455	### Parallel Opportunities After Phase 3
456	
457	```
458	Phase 3 (Migrate 1.x)  â”€â†’  Phase 3a (Rule Schema)  â”€â†’  Phase 3b (Audit)
459	                                                     â”€â†’  Phase 4 (Migrate 2.0)
460	Phase 3c (URI Fixup) can start after Phase 1 (parallel with Phase 2/3)
461	Phase 3c BLOCKS Phase 4 concrete rules (participantsâ†’subjects needs URI fixup)
462	```
463	
464	Earlier parallel opportunities (after Phase 1) remain:
465	
466	```
467	Phase 2 (Rename)  â”€â†’  Phase 3 (Migrate 1.x)  â”€â†’  Phase 3a â”€â†’ Phase 3b, Phase 4
468	                  â”€â†’  Phase 5 (Subject)       â”€â†’  Phase 9 (Merge)
469	                  â”€â†’  Phase 6 (Session)       â”€â†’
470	                  â”€â†’  Phase 8 (Remove)
471	Phase 3c (URI Fixup) can start after Phase 1 (parallel with everything)
472	Phase 7 (Metadata) can start immediately after Phase 1
473	Phase 10 (Split) can start immediately after Phase 1
474	Phase 11 (Completion) can start immediately after Phase 1
475	```
476	
477	### Within Each Phase
478	
479	- Tests MUST be written and FAIL before implementation (TDD per constitution)
480	- Models/types before services
481	- Library before CLI
482	- Commit after each task or logical group
483	
484	## Implementation Strategy
485	
486	### MVP First (Stories 1-2)
487	
488	1. Complete Phase 0: Scaffolding
489	2. Complete Phase 1: Infrastructure (CRITICAL â€” blocks everything)
490	3. Complete Phase 2: Rename (US1) â†’ **validate independently**
491	4. Complete Phase 3: Migrate 1.x (US2) â†’ **validate independently**
492	5. Ship: `bids-utils rename` + `bids-utils migrate` cover the highest-priority needs
493	
494	### Incremental Delivery
495	
496	Each subsequent phase adds value without breaking prior phases:
497	- Phase 3a adds tiered migration levels (safe/advisory/non-auto-fixable)
498	- Phase 3b adds schema audit (catches drift when bidsschematools updates)
499	- Phase 3c adds BIDS URI fixup (reusable across all rename operations)
500	- Phase 4 adds concrete 2.0 migration (participantsâ†’subjects)
501	- Phases 5-6 add subject/session rename
502	- Phase 7 adds metadata management
503	- Phases 8-10 add remove/merge/split
504	
505	---
506	
507	## Notes
508	
509	- [P] tasks = different files, no dependencies â€” can run in parallel
510	- [Story] label maps task to specific user story for traceability
511	- Each user story is independently completable and testable
512	- Verify tests fail before implementing (TDD â€” constitution Principle V)
513	- Commit after each task or logical group
514	- Stop at any checkpoint to validate story independently
515

# Tasks: bids-utils â€” Core Library & CLI

**Input**: Design documents from `/specs/000-initial-design/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md, spec (000-initial-design.md), research.md, data-model.md, contracts/library-api.md

## Format: `[ID] [P?] [Story] Description`

- **[P]**: Can run in parallel (different files, no dependencies)
- **[Story]**: Which user story this task belongs to (e.g., US1, US2, US3)
- Include exact file paths in descriptions

---

## Phase 0: Project Scaffolding

**Purpose**: Working project skeleton with CI, linting, type checking, and an empty CLI.

- [X] T001 Initialize project with `uv`: create `pyproject.toml` with dependency layers (`test`/`devel`/`ci`), package metadata, `[project.scripts]` entry point for `bids-utils` CLI
- [X] T002 Create `tox.ini` with envs: `py310`â€“`py314`, `lint`, `type`, `duplication`; configure `tox-gh-actions` mapping
- [X] T003 [P] Set up GitHub Actions CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) â€” install `.[ci]`, run `tox`
- [X] T004 [P] Create `src/bids_utils/__init__.py` with `__version__`
- [X] T005 [P] Create `src/bids_utils/cli/__init__.py` with `click` group entry point (`bids-utils --help` works)
- [X] T006 [P] Add `bids-examples` as a git submodule for testing
- [X] T007 [P] Configure `mkdocs.yml` with basic documentation structure
- [X] T008 [P] Set up intuit/auto for automated releases (`.autorc`, labels)
- [X] T009 [P] Create `tests/conftest.py` with shared fixtures (tmp BIDS dataset factory, `bids-examples` path helper)
- [X] T010 Verify: `tox` passes, `bids-utils --help` works, CI green

**Checkpoint**: Project skeleton is functional, CI is green, CLI prints help.

---

## Phase 1: Core Infrastructure (Private Modules)

**Purpose**: Shared utilities that ALL commands depend on. BLOCKS all user story work.

- [X] T011 Implement `src/bids_utils/_types.py`: `Entity` (frozen dataclass: key+value), `BIDSPath` (entities dict, suffix, extension, datatype; `from_path()`, `to_filename()`, `to_relative_path()`, `with_entities()`, `with_suffix()`, `with_extension()`), `OperationResult`, `Change` dataclasses per data-model.md
- [X] T012 [P] Write tests for `_types.py` in `tests/test_types.py` â€” entity parsing, filename round-tripping, `BIDSPath.from_path()` with various BIDS filenames
- [X] T123 [US1] Fix `BIDSPath.to_filename()` in `src/bids_utils/_types.py` to emit entities in schema-defined order (FR-035). When a `BIDSSchema` is available, `to_filename()` MUST reorder entities according to `BIDSSchema.entity_order()` before assembling the filename. Without a schema, preserve insertion order (best-effort). Add test in `tests/test_types.py`: `BIDSPath(entities={"sub":"01","task":"rest","run":"99","recording":"bipolar"}).to_filename(schema=...)` produces entities in schema order, not insertion order.
- [X] T013 Implement `src/bids_utils/_dataset.py`: `BIDSDataset` dataclass (`root`, `bids_version`, `schema_version`, `vcs`), `BIDSDataset.from_path()` (walk up to find `dataset_description.json`), read `BIDSVersion`
- [X] T014 [P] Write tests for `_dataset.py` in `tests/test_dataset.py` â€” discovery from nested paths, missing `dataset_description.json`, version extraction
- [X] T015 Implement `src/bids_utils/_schema.py`: `BIDSSchema` class wrapping `bidsschematools.schema.load_schema()` â€” load by version, `entity_order()`, `sidecar_extensions(suffix)`, `is_valid_entity()`, `deprecation_rules(from_ver, to_ver)`, `metadata_field_info()`
- [X] T016 [P] Write tests for `_schema.py` in `tests/test_schema.py` â€” schema loading, entity queries, sidecar extension queries, deprecation rule extraction
- [X] T017 Implement `src/bids_utils/_vcs.py`: `VCSBackend` protocol, `NoVCS`, `Git`, `GitAnnex`, `DataLad` implementations with `move()`, `remove()`, `is_dirty()`, `commit()`. Detection order: DataLad â†’ GitAnnex â†’ Git â†’ NoVCS
- [X] T018 [P] Write tests for `_vcs.py` in `tests/test_vcs.py` â€” detection logic, `git mv` integration, fallback to filesystem ops
- [X] T019 Implement `src/bids_utils/_sidecars.py`: given a BIDS file path + schema, find all associated sidecars by replacing extension with each known sidecar extension
- [X] T020 [P] Write tests for `_sidecars.py` in `tests/test_sidecars.py` â€” sidecar discovery for `.nii.gz` with `.json`, `.bvec`, `.bval`; missing sidecars; inheritance-level sidecars
- [X] T021 Implement `src/bids_utils/_scans.py`: read/write `_scans.tsv`, find scans file for a given file, update/remove entries by filename
- [X] T022 [P] Write tests for `_scans.py` in `tests/test_scans.py` â€” read/write round-trip, entry update, entry removal, missing `_scans.tsv`
- [X] T023 Implement `src/bids_utils/_participants.py`: read/write `participants.tsv`, add/remove/rename subject entries
- [X] T024 [P] Write tests for `_participants.py` in `tests/test_participants.py` â€” CRUD operations, duplicate detection

**Checkpoint**: All private infrastructure modules pass tests. No user-facing features yet.

---

## Phase 1b: Annexed Content Handling (FR-022)

**Purpose**: Content-aware I/O layer so all commands work correctly on git-annex/DataLad datasets where file content may not be locally available. Retroactively completes the VCS integration promise from Phase 1.

**Independent Test**: Run `bids-utils --annexed=get session-rename` on a DataLad dataset with annexed `_scans.tsv` â€” content is auto-fetched, rename succeeds.

### Foundation

- [X] T086 Add `AnnexedMode` enum (`error`, `get`, `skip-warning`, `skip`) and `ContentNotAvailableError` exception to `src/bids_utils/_types.py`. Add `annexed_mode: AnnexedMode` field to `BIDSDataset` in `src/bids_utils/_dataset.py` (default: `AnnexedMode.ERROR`).
- [X] T087 Extend `VCSBackend` protocol in `src/bids_utils/_vcs.py` with four new methods: `has_content(path: Path) -> bool`, `get_content(paths: list[Path]) -> None` for reads; `unlock(paths: list[Path]) -> None`, `add(paths: list[Path]) -> None` for writes. Implement for all backends: `NoVCS` all no-op/True; `Git` has_content=True, unlock=no-op, add=`git add`; `GitAnnex` checks symlink target, runs `git annex get/unlock/add`; `DataLad` uses `datalad get/unlock`, `git annex add`.

### Content-aware I/O layer

- [X] T088 Create `src/bids_utils/_io.py` with: `ensure_content(path, vcs, annexed_mode)` enforcing `--annexed` policy for reads; `ensure_writable(path, vcs)` calling `vcs.unlock()` for locked annexed files before writes (always, independent of `--annexed` mode); `mark_modified(paths, vcs)` calling `vcs.add()` after writes to re-annex; `read_json(path, vcs, mode) -> dict | None` and `write_json(path, data, vcs)` helpers.
- [X] T089 Wire content-aware I/O through existing code: update `_tsv.read_tsv`/`write_tsv` to accept optional `vcs`/`annexed_mode` params; update callers in `_scans.py`, `_participants.py`, `session.py`, `subject.py`, `rename.py` to pass `dataset.vcs`/`dataset.annexed_mode`. Replace inline `json.loads(f.read_text())` in `metadata.py` and `migrate.py` with `_io.read_json()`. Replace inline `f.write_text(json.dumps(...))` with `_io.write_json()` (which brackets with ensure_writable/mark_modified).

### CLI wiring

- [X] T090 Add `--annexed` option to CLI group in `src/bids_utils/cli/__init__.py` (with `envvar="BIDS_UTILS_ANNEXED"`). Update `load_dataset()` in `_common.py` to set `annexed_mode` on the returned `BIDSDataset` from Click context. All existing subcommands inherit automatically.

### Tests

- [X] T091 Write tests: `tests/test_io.py` for `ensure_content`/`ensure_writable`/`mark_modified`/`read_json`/`write_json` with all four annexed modes using mock VCS; `tests/test_vcs.py` additions for `has_content`/`get_content`/`unlock`/`add` on all backends; `tests/test_cli_common.py` additions for `--annexed` group option flow and env var; integration test with actual git-annex repo (locked files: read requires get+unlock, write unlocks then re-adds).

**Checkpoint**: `bids-utils --annexed=get session-rename` works on a git-annex dataset â€” content is fetched, locked files are unlocked for modification, and re-annexed after writes. All existing tests still pass. `--annexed=error` gives an informative error pointing to `--annexed=get`.

---

## Phase 2: User Story 1 â€” Rename a BIDS File (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: `bids-utils rename` working end-to-end â€” rename a file and all its sidecars, update `_scans.tsv`, use VCS when present.

**Independent Test**: Rename a file in any `bids-examples` dataset, run BIDS validator, confirm validity.

### Implementation for User Story 1

- [X] T025 [US1] Implement `src/bids_utils/rename.py`: `rename_file()` per library-api.md contract â€” parse source into `BIDSPath`, apply entity overrides, compute new filename, discover sidecars, check for conflicts, execute renames (filesystem or VCS), update `_scans.tsv`
- [X] T026 [US1] Write tests for `rename.py` in `tests/test_rename.py`:
  - Rename with entity override (`--set task=nback`) renames file + sidecars
  - `_scans.tsv` entry updated after rename
  - Conflict detection (target already exists â†’ error)
  - Non-BIDS filenames (e.g., `_bold__dup-01.json`) handled gracefully
  - Dry-run returns changes without modifying files
  - VCS (`git mv`) used when in git repo
- [X] T027 [US1] Implement `src/bids_utils/cli/rename.py`: click command wiring `--set`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `-v`/`-q`
- [X] T028 [US1] Implement `src/bids_utils/cli/_common.py`: shared CLI decorators/options (`--dry-run`, `--json`, `-v`/`-q`, `--force`, `--include-sourcedata`, `--schema-version`)
- [X] T029 [US1] Write CLI smoke tests in `tests/test_cli.py` â€” `bids-utils rename --help`, `bids-utils rename --dry-run` on a fixture dataset
- [X] T030 [US1] Write `bids-examples` sweep test in `tests/integration/test_bids_examples.py` â€” rename a random file in each dataset, validate

**Checkpoint**: `bids-utils rename` is functional. Single-file rename with sidecars, scans, VCS all working.

---

## Phase 3: User Story 2 â€” Migrate Dataset within BIDS 1.x (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: `bids-utils migrate` resolves all 1.x deprecations using schema-derived rules.

**Independent Test**: Take a BIDS 1.4-era dataset, run `bids-utils migrate`, verify deprecation warnings eliminated.

### Implementation for User Story 2

- [X] T031 [US2] Implement migration rule engine in `src/bids_utils/migrate.py`: `MigrationRule`, `MigrationPlan`, `MigrationFinding` dataclasses per data-model.md; migration registry (decorator-based, adapted from PR #2282 pattern); load deprecation rules from schema (`rules/checks/deprecations.yml`, `objects/metadata.yaml`, `objects/enums.yaml`)
- [X] T032 [US2] Implement metadata field rename handler: `BasedOn` â†’ `Sources`, `RawSources` â†’ `Sources`. Merge logic MUST handle: (a) existing `Sources` as string + incoming value as array (normalize to array first), (b) both `BasedOn` AND `RawSources` present alongside existing `Sources` (3-way merge). Add tests for mixed-type merge and 3-way merge scenarios. (Note: `ScanDate` handled by T036; `DCOffsetCorrection` by T105; `AcquisitionDuration` by T104.) *(Reimplementation needed: current merge logic broken for mixed string/array types.)*
- [X] T033 [US2] Implement value format change handler: relative paths â†’ BIDS URIs in `IntendedFor`, `AssociatedEmptyRoom`, `Sources`; `DatasetDOI` bare DOIs â†’ URI format
- [X] T034 [US2] Implement suffix deprecation handler: `_phase` â†’ `_part-phase_bold`; deprecated anat suffixes `T2star`, `FLASH`, `PD` (delegates to `rename_file()`)
- [X] T035 [US2] Implement enum value rename handler: `ElektaNeuromag` â†’ `NeuromagElektaMEGIN`, deprecated template identifiers (`fsaverage3`â€“`fsaverage6`, `fsaveragesym`, versioned `UNCInfant*`)
- [X] T036 [US2] Implement cross-file move handler: `ScanDate` from JSON sidecar â†’ `acq_time` column in `_scans.tsv`. MUST create `_scans.tsv` with appropriate headers if it does not exist â€” current implementation silently skips the TSV write when the file is missing, causing data loss (`ScanDate` removed from JSON but never written to TSV). Add test for missing-`_scans.tsv` scenario. *(Reimplementation needed: data loss bug.)*
- [X] T037 [US2] Implement `migrate_dataset()` orchestrator: determine dataset version, determine target version (default: current released 1.x), compute applicable rules between versions, scan dataset for findings, apply auto-fixable findings, report unfixable ones
- [X] T038 [US2] Write tests for `migrate.py` in `tests/test_migrate.py`:
  - Metadata field renames applied correctly
  - Relative paths converted to BIDS URIs
  - Suffix deprecations trigger file renames
  - Enum values updated
  - `ScanDate` moved to `_scans.tsv`
  - `--dry-run` lists findings without modifying
  - Already-compliant dataset â†’ "nothing to do"
